target_outcome: Build a repeatable pipeline of qualified buyers for optical payload programs. Specifically: get in front of buyers who can move without strict flight heritage requirements — commercial EO operators, defense prime subcontractors, R&D-funded programs — before competitors do.
offer: Optical payload hardware for small satellites. Fast integration cycles, compact SWaP. No flight heritage requirement on the buyer side.
constraints: Flight heritage objection blocks deals with large primes and government programs
must target segments where heritage is not a hard requirement
Peregrine has real hardware in the pipeline and a meaningful contract but is early stage
Buyer is often VP Mission Systems, Chief Engineer, or Program Manager — not a sales team
known_context: Peregrine Space builds optical payloads and related infrastructure. Early stage, real traction including at least one meaningful contract. Moving upmarket runs into flight heritage objection. Key insight: target buyers and partners who can move without strict heritage requirements — commercial NewSpace, defense prime subcontracting, RD-funded programs. Founder Tyler is engaged.
